skill.name: gws-drive-upload
skill.description: Google Drive: Upload a file with automatic metadata.
skill.summary: Upload files to Google Drive with automatic metadata detection using the Google Workspace CLI.
skill.author: googleworkspace

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 14 patterns (external_commands, filesystem, cryptographic, network) but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file is documentation-only markdown describing the gws CLI tool usage. Path references like '../gws-shared/SKILL.md' are documentation links, not filesystem operations. No executable code or security risks present.

content.value_statement: Quickly upload files to Google Drive with automatic metadata detection. Uses the Google Workspace CLI to handle MIME types and file information without manual configuration.
actual_capabilities: Upload files from local filesystem to Google Drive
Automatically detect and set MIME type metadata
Specify custom parent folder for uploaded files
Rename files during upload with the --name flag
Preserve original filename when no custom name provided
limitations: Requires gws CLI tool to be installed and authenticated
Cannot upload folders, only individual files
Requires valid Google Workspace authentication credentials
Subject to Google Drive API quota limits and file size restrictions

use_cases.title: Developer backup workflow
use_cases.description: Automatically upload configuration files, logs, or build artifacts to a designated Drive folder for team access and backup.

use_cases.title: Document sharing for teams
use_cases.description: Upload reports, presentations, or data files to shared team drives with proper folder organization.

use_cases.title: Automated file pipelines
use_cases.description: Integrate file uploads into automated workflows where generated files need to be stored in Google Drive.

prompt_templates.title: Basic file upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload the file report.pdf to my Google Drive
prompt_templates.scenario: Simple single file upload with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Upload to specific folder
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload data.csv to the folder with ID 1ABC123xyz
prompt_templates.scenario: Upload a file to a specific parent folder

prompt_templates.title: Upload with custom filename
prompt_templates.scenario: Rename file during upload for clarity

prompt_templates.title: Batch upload workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload all PDF files from the current directory to the Reports folder, confirming each upload
prompt_templates.scenario: Multiple file uploads with user confirmation

output_examples.input: Upload quarterly-report.pdf to the Finance folder
output_examples.output: Executing: gws drive +upload ./quarterly-report.pdf --parent FINANCE_FOLDER_ID. File uploaded successfully with MIME type application/pdf
Executing: gws drive +upload ./data.csv --name Sales_Data_2024.csv. Upload complete. File available in My Drive

best_practices: Always confirm file uploads with the user before executing, as this is a write operation
Verify the correct parent folder ID before uploading to avoid misplacing files
Use descriptive filenames with the --name flag when the source filename is unclear
anti_patterns: Uploading files without confirming the target location with the user
Using vague or generic filenames that make files hard to find later
Uploading sensitive files to shared drives without verifying permissions

faq.question: Do I need to install additional software to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the Google Workspace CLI (gws) must be installed and authenticated. Run 'gws auth login' to set up credentials.

faq.question: Can I upload entire folders?
faq.answer: No, this command uploads individual files only. For folders, you would need to upload files individually or use the Google Drive web interface.

faq.question: How is the MIME type determined?
faq.answer: The gws CLI automatically detects the MIME type based on the file extension and content. You do not need to specify it manually.

faq.question: What happens if I do not specify a parent folder?
faq.answer: The file will be uploaded to the root of your Google Drive (My Drive). Use the --parent flag to target a specific folder.

faq.question: Can I overwrite an existing file with the same name?
faq.answer: Google Drive treats uploads as new files by default. To replace an existing file, you would need to use the file ID with a different command.

faq.question: Is there a file size limit for uploads?
faq.answer: Google Drive has a 5 TB per-file limit. The gws CLI handles large files with resumable uploads, but network stability may affect very large transfers.
